description: Fix unguarded JSON.parse() calls in run_report and run_realtime_report tools that throw native SyntaxError when the AI passes invalid JSON strings, instead of a structured ToolError.validation().
qualityChecks: cd plugins/google-analytics && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Wrap JSON.parse calls in run_report with try-catch
userStories.description: In plugins/google-analytics/src/tools/run-report.ts, the dimension_filter, metric_filter, and order_by parameters are passed directly to JSON.parse() at lines 81, 84, and 87 without try-catch. When the AI provides invalid JSON, a native SyntaxError escapes instead of a ToolError.validation(). Wrap each JSON.parse call in try-catch and throw ToolError.validation() on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON.parse calls for dimension_filter, metric_filter, and order_by in run-report.ts are each wrapped in try-catch
Invalid JSON strings throw ToolError.validation with a descriptive message identifying which parameter failed
Valid JSON strings are still parsed and passed to the API correctly

userStories.title: Wrap JSON.parse calls in run_realtime_report with try-catch
userStories.description: In plugins/google-analytics/src/tools/run-realtime-report.ts, the dimension_filter and metric_filter parameters are passed directly to JSON.parse() at lines 57 and 60 without try-catch. Apply the same fix as US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON.parse calls for dimension_filter and metric_filter in run-realtime-report.ts are each wrapped in try-catch
Invalid JSON strings throw ToolError.validation with a descriptive message
Valid JSON strings are still parsed and passed to the API correctly
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/google-analytics/src/tools/run-realtime-report.ts (wrap 2 JSON.parse calls)

Context:
Same pattern as run-report.ts — lines 57 and 60 have JSON.parse on user-provided strings.

Changes:
Same try-catch pattern as US-001, but only for dimension_filter and metric_filter.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change run-report.ts in this story (covered by US-001)
